name: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY)
description: Public health-assurance scheme that provides eligible families cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals for covered secondary and tertiary care.

benefit.amount: Up to INR 5 lakh per family per year
benefit.description: Cashless cover for eligible hospitalisation packages at empanelled public and private hospitals, subject to current scheme rules.

other_conditions: Beneficiary eligibility must be confirmed in the official PM-JAY system; participating States and Union Territories may use their own implementation rules.

benefits.financial: Up to INR 5 lakh cashless hospitalisation cover per family per year

ai_explanation.summary: Cashless hospitalisation support for families listed as eligible under PM-JAY.
ai_explanation.eligibility_reason_template: Use the official beneficiary search to confirm your family record and nearby empanelled hospitals.
tips: Confirm empanelled-hospital availability before seeking planned treatment.
next_steps: Contact a nearby empanelled hospital help desk.

name: Rashtriya Bal Swasthya Karyakram
description: Child health-screening and early-intervention programme for children from birth to 18 years, delivered through public health and school systems.

benefit.description: Provides screening for health conditions, deficiencies, diseases, and developmental delays, with referral for follow-up care where required.

other_conditions: Available through designated public health facilities, Anganwadi centres, and government or government-aided schools under local implementation arrangements.

required_documents: Child Birth Certificate or Age Proof

ai_metadata.estimated_processing_time: Depends on local screening and referral schedule

ai_explanation.summary: Early screening and referral support for children with identified health or developmental needs.
ai_explanation.eligibility_reason_template: Contact your local public health facility, Anganwadi centre, or school health team for the local screening schedule.
common_rejection_reasons: Local screening round not yet scheduled
tips: Carry previous medical records to the screening or referral visit.
next_steps: Ask the nearest public health facility about RBSK services.
Attend the scheduled screening or referral appointment.

source.organization: National Health Mission, Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
